summary: Crystal‑clear studio outtakes with superb sound quality; highlights include a soaring Stella Blue, a fiery Weather Report Suite and an inspired Row Jimmy.
review: The archive provides an excellent‑sounding source—Betty Cantor’s mix is described as crisp, clean and studio‑level by virtually every reviewer. Musically the collection shines, with fans repeatedly calling out Stella Blue, Weather Report Suite, Row Jimmy, Here Comes Sunshine and Let It Grow as standout moments, while only a single track (Sing Your Blues Away) draws criticism.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 detailed five‑star reviews praise both the audio fidelity and the performances; only one track receives a negative comment, leading to an overall rating well above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers repeatedly note the mix as crisp, clean, studio‑level with no hiss, drop‑outs or muffiness; Betty Cantor’s engineering is highlighted as a major strength.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The outtakes flow like a curated mini‑album, showcasing the band’s mid‑70s eclecticism—from bluesy ballads to jazzy suites—while giving listeners insight into song development and alternate arrangements.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar solos and pedal‑steel work are repeatedly praised; his lead on Let It Grow and Row Jimmy is singled out as extraordinary.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned only in passing regarding possible vocal harmonies; no specific instrumental commentary.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s rhythm‑guitar work on Row Jimmy receives high praise, described as “extraordinary”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s funky keyboard textures and occasional vocal harmonies on Stella Blue/Here Comes Sunshine are highlighted.
